title: Apartments for Rent on the West Side of Cleveland
metaTitle: West Side Cleveland Apartments for Rent
metaDescription: Explore apartments for rent on Cleveland's west side, from Ohio City to Old Brooklyn. See neighborhoods, transit, rents, and voucher-friendly rental homes.
primaryKeyword: west side cleveland apartments for rent
answerBox: Cleveland's west side runs along and west of the Cuyahoga River and includes neighborhoods such as Ohio City, Tremont, Detroit-Shoreway, Old Brooklyn, and Kamm's Corners. Rent Finder Cleveland's selection is concentrated on the city's east side, but we also work with a smaller number of Section 8-accepting rental homes on the west side, including in Cudell/Detroit-Shoreway (44102) and Old Brooklyn/Brooklyn Centre (44109).

body: Cleveland's neighborhoods are officially organized into 34 Statistical Planning Areas by the City Planning Commission, split generally by position relative to the Cuyahoga River. The west side covers 14 of those areas, including **Ohio City** (home to the West Side Market and the RTA Red Line's Ohio City station), **Tremont** (an arts and dining district near Lincoln Park), **Detroit-Shoreway** (home to the Gordon Square Arts District), **Edgewater** (lakefront, near Edgewater Park), **Clark-Fulton** (along the MetroHealth main campus corridor), **Old Brooklyn** (near the Cleveland Metroparks Zoo), and **Kamm's Corners** and **Jefferson** in the far-west **West Park** area near Cleveland Hopkins International Airport.

Renters searching "west side Cleveland apartments" are typically comparing this stretch of neighborhoods against the city's east side, which runs from Downtown through University Circle, Hough, Glenville, and out toward the eastern suburbs. The west side's housing stock leans toward early-1900s single-family homes, duplexes, and small apartment buildings, with denser mid-rise construction concentrated closer to Ohio City, Tremont, and the Detroit-Shoreway/Gordon Square corridor.

sections.h2: What does rent look like on the west side?
body: West-side rents vary widely by neighborhood and building age. For general market context, here's what two widely used rent-tracking sources reported citywide (not west-side-specific): Zumper's median-asking-rent report (dated July 4, 2026) and RentCafe's average-rent report for professionally managed apartments (dated July 2, 2026). Individual west-side neighborhoods can run above or below these citywide figures depending on the building and block.

The **RTA Red Line**, Cleveland's heavy-rail line, runs from Cleveland Hopkins International Airport through the near-west side (including an Ohio City station) to Tower City downtown and on to the east side — Cleveland's only rapid-transit line that directly connects downtown to the city's main airport. West-side neighborhoods farther from the rail line, such as Kamm's Corners and Jefferson in the West Park area, rely more heavily on GCRTA's local bus network. I-90, I-71, and I-480 all run through or near west-side neighborhoods, giving drivers direct highway access toward downtown and the outer suburbs. Renters without a car should check the specific bus routes serving any west-side address before committing to a lease, since coverage and frequency vary by neighborhood.

sections.h2: Where Rent Finder Cleveland has rental homes on the west side
body: Our rental selection is concentrated on Cleveland's **east and southeast side** — neighborhoods like Slavic Village, Collinwood, Glenville, Fairfax, Hough, and Buckeye-Shaker — but we also work with a smaller number of homes on the west side, including in **Cudell/Detroit-Shoreway (ZIP 44102)** and **Old Brooklyn/Brooklyn Centre (ZIP 44109)**, with occasional homes in the Ohio City/Tremont area (ZIP 44113). Every home we work with, on either side of the city, accepts Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) and is HUD-inspection-ready.

Because our west-side inventory is smaller than our east-side inventory, availability in any specific west-side neighborhood shifts often. [Book a showing](/p/book-showing) and tell us which west-side area you're targeting, or browse our [general Cleveland apartments page](/apartments-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) and [houses for rent page](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) for the full current picture across the city. For west-side-specific detail, see our [Old Brooklyn neighborhood page](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-old-brooklyn-cleveland/) or [Detroit-Shoreway neighborhood page](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-detroit-shoreway-cleveland/).

faq.q: What neighborhoods are on Cleveland's west side?
faq.a: Cleveland's west side includes 14 officially designated planning areas, among them Ohio City, Tremont, Detroit-Shoreway, Edgewater, Clark-Fulton, Old Brooklyn, Kamm's Corners, and Jefferson. It generally runs along and west of the Cuyahoga River, as distinguished from the city's east side.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland works with apartments on the west side?
faq.a: Yes, though our current selection is concentrated on the east and southeast side. We work with a smaller number of Section 8-accepting rental homes on the west side, including in Cudell/Detroit-Shoreway (44102) and Old Brooklyn/Brooklyn Centre (44109). Book a showing to ask about current west-side availability.

faq.q: How do I get around Cleveland's west side without a car?
faq.a: The RTA Red Line connects Cleveland Hopkins International Airport, the near-west side (including an Ohio City station), and downtown. Neighborhoods farther from the rail line rely on GCRTA's local bus network — check specific routes for your target area.

faq.q: Is the west side cheaper than the east side of Cleveland?
faq.a: It depends on the specific neighborhood and building; both sides of the city include a range of price points. Citywide rent figures from Zumper and RentCafe (July 2026) provide a general benchmark, but individual west-side neighborhoods can run above or below those citywide numbers.

Acceptance depends on the individual landlord. Every home Rent Finder Cleveland works with — east side or west side — accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. For the wider voucher process, see our Cleveland Section 8 guide.
